---
title: "Data preprocessing helpers"
output: html_document
editor_options:
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r development, include=FALSE}
library(testthat)
```

<!--
 Source of truth for DHS-style data preprocessing helpers. Run the inflate
 chunk at the bottom after editing.
-->

```{r development-load}
# Load already included functions if relevant
pkgload::load_all(export_all = TRUE)
```

# DHS fixed-width imports and child-level preparation

```{r development-data-prep}
# Helpers adapted from thesis data preparation scripts. Authentication and
# cookie-based downloading should stay in local, non-package scripts.
```

```{r function-sas_at_to_fwf}
#' Parse SAS fixed-width input positions
#'
#' Read a DHS-style SAS input file and extract fixed-width column positions
#' from statements such as `@1 HIVCLUST 8.0` or `@16 HIV01 $6.`.
#'
#' @param sas_file Path to a SAS input file.
#'
#' @return A data frame with `start`, `end`, `width`, and `var` columns.
#'
#' @export
sas_at_to_fwf <- function(sas_file) {
  lines <- readLines(sas_file, warn = FALSE)
  pattern <- paste0(
    "^\\s*@\\s*(\\d+)\\s+",
    "([A-Za-z0-9_]+)\\s+",
    "(\\$)?\\s*(\\d+)(?:\\.|\\.\\d+)?"
  )
  matches <- regmatches(lines, regexec(pattern, lines))
  matches <- matches[lengths(matches) > 0L]

  if (!length(matches)) {
    return(data.frame(
      start = integer(),
      end = integer(),
      width = integer(),
      var = character(),
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    ))
  }

  starts <- as.integer(vapply(matches, `[[`, character(1), 2L))
  vars <- vapply(matches, `[[`, character(1), 3L)
  widths <- as.integer(vapply(matches, `[[`, character(1), 5L))

  out <- data.frame(
    start = starts,
    end = starts + widths - 1L,
    width = widths,
    var = vars,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  out[order(out$start), , drop = FALSE]
}
```

```{r function-read_dhs_zip}
#' Read a DHS zip file containing DAT and SAS files
#'
#' Unzips one DHS archive, finds the first `.DAT` and `.SAS` file, parses the
#' SAS fixed-width layout, and reads the data as character columns.
#'
#' @param zip_file Path to a DHS zip archive.
#'
#' @return A `data.table`, or `NULL` when the archive does not contain both
#'   `.DAT` and `.SAS` files.
#'
#' @export
read_dhs_zip <- function(zip_file) {
  temp_dir <- file.path(
    tempdir(),
    paste0("dhs_", Sys.getpid(), "_", sample.int(1e6, 1L))
  )
  dir.create(temp_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
  on.exit(unlink(temp_dir, recursive = TRUE), add = TRUE)

  utils::unzip(zip_file, exdir = temp_dir)
  files_in_temp <- list.files(temp_dir, full.names = TRUE, recursive = TRUE)

  dat_file <- files_in_temp[grepl("\\.DAT$", files_in_temp, ignore.case = TRUE)]
  sas_file <- files_in_temp[grepl("\\.SAS$", files_in_temp, ignore.case = TRUE)]

  if (!length(dat_file) || !length(sas_file)) {
    message(
      "  [!] Skipping ", basename(zip_file),
      ": missing .DAT or .SAS files."
    )
    return(NULL)
  }

  spec <- sas_at_to_fwf(sas_file[1L])
  if (!nrow(spec)) {
    stop(
      "No fixed-width positions found in ",
      basename(sas_file[1L]), ".",
      call. = FALSE
    )
  }

  widths <- integer()
  col_names <- character()
  next_start <- 1L
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(spec))) {
    if (spec$start[i] > next_start) {
      widths <- c(widths, -(spec$start[i] - next_start))
    }
    widths <- c(widths, spec$width[i])
    col_names <- c(col_names, spec$var[i])
    next_start <- spec$end[i] + 1L
  }

  df <- utils::read.fwf(
    file = dat_file[1L],
    widths = widths,
    col.names = col_names,
    colClasses = "character",
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE,
    strip.white = FALSE
  )

  data.table::as.data.table(df)
}
```

```{r function-build_dhs_catalog}
#' Build a catalog of downloaded DHS files
#'
#' @param zip_dir Directory containing downloaded DHS zip archives.
#'
#' @return A `data.table` with parsed country, module type, phase, version, and
#'   survey ID columns.
#'
#' @export
build_dhs_catalog <- function(zip_dir = file.path("data", "downloaded")) {
  zips <- list.files(
    zip_dir,
    pattern = "\\.zip$",
    full.names = TRUE,
    ignore.case = TRUE
  )
  if (!length(zips)) {
    return(data.table::data.table())
  }

  catalog <- data.table::data.table(
    file_path = zips,
    base_name = basename(zips)
  )
  catalog$file_stub <- tools::file_path_sans_ext(catalog$base_name)
  catalog$country <- substr(catalog$file_stub, 1L, 2L)
  catalog$type <- substr(catalog$file_stub, 3L, 4L)
  catalog$phase <- substr(catalog$file_stub, 5L, 5L)
  catalog$version <- substr(catalog$file_stub, 6L, 6L)
  catalog$survey_id <- paste0(catalog$phase, catalog$version)

  catalog
}
```

```{r function-standardize_dhs_ids}
#' Standardize DHS ID columns for merging
#'
#' DHS modules use different variable names for household, person, mother,
#' father, and child IDs. This helper normalizes the ID names used by the merge
#' helpers in this file.
#'
#' @param dt A rectangular data object.
#' @param dtype DHS module type, such as `"KR"`, `"BR"`, `"IR"`, or `"HR"`.
#'
#' @return A `data.table` with lower-case names and standardized ID columns.
#'
#' @export
standardize_dhs_ids <- function(dt, dtype) {
  dt_copy <- data.table::copy(data.table::as.data.table(dt))
  data.table::setnames(dt_copy, tolower(names(dt_copy)))

  dtype <- toupper(dtype)
  rename_map <- list(
    HR = c(hv001 = "cluster_id", hv002 = "household_id", hvidx = "line_id"),
    PR = c(hv001 = "cluster_id", hv002 = "household_id", hvidx = "line_id"),
    CR = c(
      v001 = "cluster_id",
      v002 = "household_id",
      v003 = "mother_line_id",
      mv001 = "father_cluster_id",
      mv002 = "father_household_id",
      mv003 = "father_line_id"
    ),
    IR = c(v001 = "cluster_id", v002 = "household_id", v003 = "line_id"),
    MR = c(mv001 = "cluster_id", mv002 = "household_id", mv003 = "line_id"),
    KR = c(
      v001 = "cluster_id",
      v002 = "household_id",
      v003 = "mother_line_id",
      b16 = "child_line_id"
    ),
    BR = c(
      v001 = "cluster_id",
      v002 = "household_id",
      v003 = "mother_line_id",
      b16 = "child_line_id"
    ),
    AR = c(
      hivclust = "cluster_id",
      hivnumb = "household_id",
      hivline = "line_id"
    )
  )

  if (!dtype %in% names(rename_map)) {
    warning(sprintf("Unsupported DHS module type: %s", dtype), call. = FALSE)
    return(dt_copy)
  }

  map <- rename_map[[dtype]]
  old_names <- intersect(names(map), names(dt_copy))
  if (length(old_names)) {
    data.table::setnames(dt_copy, old_names, unname(map[old_names]))
  }

  id_cols <- intersect(
    c(
      "cluster_id",
      "household_id",
      "line_id",
      "mother_line_id",
      "child_line_id",
      "father_cluster_id",
      "father_household_id",
      "father_line_id"
    ),
    names(dt_copy)
  )
  for (col in id_cols) {
    data.table::set(dt_copy, j = col, value = as.integer(dt_copy[[col]]))
  }

  dt_copy
}
```

```{r function-prep-merge-helpers}
#' Drop duplicate payload columns before joining DHS modules
#'
#' @param dt A `data.table` to be merged into another table.
#' @param existing_names Column names already present in the target table.
#' @param by_cols Join key columns that must be kept.
#'
#' @return A `data.table` with duplicate non-key columns removed.
#'
#' @keywords internal
#' @noRd
.drop_duplicate_payload <- function(dt, existing_names, by_cols) {
  duplicate_names <- intersect(setdiff(names(dt), by_cols), existing_names)
  keep_names <- setdiff(names(dt), duplicate_names)
  dt[, keep_names, with = FALSE]
}

#' Merge two DHS modules when all join keys are available
#'
#' @param x Left-hand `data.table`.
#' @param y Right-hand `data.table`.
#' @param by_x Key columns in `x`.
#' @param by_y Key columns in `y`. Defaults to `by_x`.
#' @param suffix Suffix passed to `merge()`.
#'
#' @return The merged `data.table`, or `x` unchanged when required keys are
#'   missing.
#'
#' @keywords internal
#' @noRd
.merge_if_keys <- function(x, y, by_x, by_y = by_x, suffix = "") {
  if (is.null(x) || is.null(y)) {
    return(x)
  }
  if (!all(by_x %in% names(x)) || !all(by_y %in% names(y))) {
    return(x)
  }
  y_use <- .drop_duplicate_payload(y, names(x), by_y)
  merge(
    x,
    y_use,
    by.x = by_x,
    by.y = by_y,
    all.x = TRUE,
    suffixes = c("", suffix)
  )
}
```

```{r function-dictionary_vars_for_dt}
#' Build a safe column list from a DHS dictionary
#'
#' Returns only dictionary variables that exist in a prepared data table. This
#' is useful when a shared dictionary spans multiple DHS modules but the
#' analytic table contains only a subset of them.
#'
#' @param dt A data table or data frame.
#' @param dict_dt A dictionary table with a `variable_name` column.
#' @param id_cols ID columns to include when available.
#' @param warn_missing If `TRUE`, message about requested variables not present
#'   in `dt`.
#'
#' @return A character vector of available column names.
#'
#' @export
dictionary_vars_for_dt <- function(dt,
                                   dict_dt,
                                   id_cols = c(
                                     "cluster_id",
                                     "household_id",
                                     "line_id",
                                     "mother_line_id",
                                     "child_line_id"
                                   ),
                                   warn_missing = TRUE) {
  dt <- data.table::as.data.table(dt)
  dict_dt <- data.table::as.data.table(dict_dt)

  if (!"variable_name" %in% names(dict_dt)) {
    stop("`dict_dt` must include a `variable_name` column.", call. = FALSE)
  }

  requested <- unique(c(id_cols, dict_dt[["variable_name"]]))
  available <- intersect(requested, names(dt))
  missing <- setdiff(requested, names(dt))

  if (isTRUE(warn_missing) && length(missing)) {
    message(
      sprintf(
        "Dropping %d dictionary variables not present in the dataset: %s",
        length(missing),
        paste(utils::head(missing, 10L), collapse = ", ")
      ),
      if (length(missing) > 10L) " ..."
    )
  }

  available
}
```

```{r function-prepare_country_phase}
#' Prepare a child-level DHS dataset for one country and survey release
#'
#' Builds a child-focused analytic table. The master table is anchored on KR
#' when available, otherwise BR, and can be enriched with CR, IR, HR, PR, and
#' AR modules when join keys are present.
#'
#' @param target_country Two-letter DHS country code.
#' @param target_phase DHS phase code.
#' @param target_version Optional survey version. Required when multiple
#'   releases exist for the same country and phase.
#' @param zip_dir Directory containing downloaded DHS zip archives.
#'
#' @return A list with `catalog`, `raw`, and `master` elements, or `NULL` when
#'   no files are available for the requested country and phase.
#'
#' @export
prepare_country_phase <- function(target_country,
                                  target_phase,
                                  target_version = NULL,
                                  zip_dir = file.path("data", "downloaded")) {
  catalog <- build_dhs_catalog(zip_dir)
  phase_files <- catalog[
    catalog$country == target_country &
      catalog$phase == target_phase,
  ]

  if (!nrow(phase_files)) {
    message(sprintf(
      "No files found for %s phase %s",
      target_country, target_phase
    ))
    return(NULL)
  }

  analysis_types <- c("AR", "BR", "CR", "HR", "IR", "KR", "MR", "PR")
  survey_candidates <- phase_files[phase_files$type %in% analysis_types, ]
  if (!nrow(survey_candidates)) {
    survey_candidates <- phase_files
  }

  available_surveys <- sort(unique(survey_candidates$survey_id))
  if (is.null(target_version)) {
    if (length(available_surveys) > 1L) {
      stop(
        sprintf(
          paste0(
            "Multiple survey versions found for %s phase %s: %s. ",
            "Please specify `target_version` so releases are not mixed."
          ),
          target_country,
          target_phase,
          paste(available_surveys, collapse = ", ")
        ),
        call. = FALSE
      )
    }
    target_version <- unique(phase_files$version)
  }

  selected_survey <- paste0(target_phase, target_version)
  phase_files <- phase_files[phase_files$survey_id == selected_survey, ]

  if (!nrow(phase_files)) {
    stop(
      sprintf(
        "No files found for %s survey %s.",
        target_country,
        selected_survey
      ),
      call. = FALSE
    )
  }

  message("==========================================================")
  message(sprintf(
    "Processing Country: %s  |  Phase: %s  |  Version: %s",
    target_country,
    target_phase,
    target_version
  ))
  message("==========================================================")

  data_list <- list()
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(phase_files))) {
    f_path <- phase_files$file_path[i]
    f_type <- phase_files$type[i]

    message("  -> Reading module: ", f_type, " from ", phase_files$base_name[i])
    dt <- read_dhs_zip(f_path)
    if (!is.null(dt)) {
      data_list[[f_type]] <- standardize_dhs_ids(dt, f_type)
    }
  }

  has_KR <- "KR" %in% names(data_list)
  has_BR <- "BR" %in% names(data_list)
  has_CR <- "CR" %in% names(data_list)
  has_IR <- "IR" %in% names(data_list)
  has_HR <- "HR" %in% names(data_list)
  has_PR <- "PR" %in% names(data_list)
  has_AR <- "AR" %in% names(data_list)

  if (has_KR) {
    merged_dt <- data.table::copy(data_list[["KR"]])
    base_module <- "KR"
  } else if (has_BR) {
    merged_dt <- data.table::copy(data_list[["BR"]])
    base_module <- "BR"
  } else {
    stop(
      sprintf(
        "No KR or BR child recode found for %s survey %s.",
        target_country,
        selected_survey
      ),
      call. = FALSE
    )
  }

  merged_dt$survey_country <- target_country
  merged_dt$survey_phase <- target_phase
  merged_dt$survey_version <- target_version
  merged_dt$survey_id <- selected_survey
  merged_dt$base_module <- base_module

  if (has_BR && base_module != "BR") {
    message("  -> Adding birth history variables (BR) to the child base...")
    merged_dt <- .merge_if_keys(
      merged_dt,
      data_list[["BR"]],
      by_x = c("caseid", "bidx"),
      by_y = c("caseid", "bidx"),
      suffix = "_br"
    )
  }

  if (has_CR) {
    message(
      "  -> Adding couples/parent context variables (CR) to the child base..."
    )
    merged_dt <- .merge_if_keys(
      merged_dt,
      data_list[["CR"]],
      by_x = c("cluster_id", "household_id", "mother_line_id"),
      by_y = c("cluster_id", "household_id", "mother_line_id"),
      suffix = "_cr"
    )
  }

  if (has_IR) {
    message(
      "  -> Adding maternal interview variables (IR) to the child base..."
    )
    merged_dt <- .merge_if_keys(
      merged_dt,
      data_list[["IR"]],
      by_x = c("cluster_id", "household_id", "mother_line_id"),
      by_y = c("cluster_id", "household_id", "line_id"),
      suffix = "_ir"
    )
  }

  if (has_HR) {
    message("  -> Adding household characteristics (HR) to the child base...")
    merged_dt <- .merge_if_keys(
      merged_dt,
      data_list[["HR"]],
      by_x = c("cluster_id", "household_id"),
      by_y = c("cluster_id", "household_id"),
      suffix = "_hr"
    )
  }

  if (has_PR && "child_line_id" %in% names(merged_dt)) {
    message(
      "  -> Adding household member roster variables (PR) for the child..."
    )
    merged_dt <- .merge_if_keys(
      merged_dt,
      data_list[["PR"]],
      by_x = c("cluster_id", "household_id", "child_line_id"),
      by_y = c("cluster_id", "household_id", "line_id"),
      suffix = "_pr"
    )
  }

  if (has_AR && "child_line_id" %in% names(merged_dt)) {
    message(
      "  -> Adding HIV testing variables (AR) where child line IDs match..."
    )
    merged_dt <- .merge_if_keys(
      merged_dt,
      data_list[["AR"]],
      by_x = c("cluster_id", "household_id", "child_line_id"),
      by_y = c("cluster_id", "household_id", "line_id"),
      suffix = "_ar"
    )
  }

  message(sprintf(
    "  => Final merged dataset has %d rows and %d columns",
    nrow(merged_dt),
    ncol(merged_dt)
  ))

  list(
    catalog = phase_files,
    raw = data_list,
    master = merged_dt
  )
}
```

```{r example-data-prep}
sas_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".SAS")
writeLines(c("@1 HIVCLUST 8.0", "@16 HIV01 $6."), sas_file)
sas_at_to_fwf(sas_file)
```

```{r tests-data-prep}
test_that("sas_at_to_fwf parses DHS SAS positions", {
  sas_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".SAS")
  writeLines(c(
    "INPUT",
    "  @1 HIVCLUST 8.0",
    "  @16 HIV01 $6.",
    ";"
  ), sas_file)

  expect_equal(
    sas_at_to_fwf(sas_file),
    data.frame(
      start = c(1L, 16L),
      end = c(8L, 21L),
      width = c(8L, 6L),
      var = c("HIVCLUST", "HIV01"),
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  )
})

test_that("standardize_dhs_ids normalizes module-specific IDs", {
  dt <- data.frame(
    V001 = "10",
    V002 = "20",
    V003 = "3",
    B16 = "4",
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )

  out <- standardize_dhs_ids(dt, "KR")

  expect_equal(
    names(out),
    c("cluster_id", "household_id", "mother_line_id", "child_line_id")
  )
  expect_equal(out$cluster_id, 10L)
  expect_equal(out$child_line_id, 4L)
})

test_that("dictionary_vars_for_dt keeps only available variables", {
  dt <- data.frame(
    cluster_id = 1L,
    household_id = 2L,
    wealth = 3,
    outcome = 1
  )
  dict <- data.frame(
    variable_name = c("wealth", "missing_var", "outcome"),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )

  expect_message(
    out <- dictionary_vars_for_dt(dt, dict),
    "Dropping"
  )
  expect_equal(out, c("cluster_id", "household_id", "wealth", "outcome"))
})

test_that("merge helper drops duplicate non-key payload columns", {
  x <- data.table::data.table(id = 1:2, keep = c("a", "b"))
  y <- data.table::data.table(id = 1:2, keep = c("x", "y"), add = c(3, 4))

  out <- .merge_if_keys(x, y, by_x = "id")

  expect_equal(names(out), c("id", "keep", "add"))
  expect_equal(out$add, c(3, 4))
})
```

```{r development-inflate, eval=FALSE}
# Run but keep eval=FALSE to avoid infinite loop
# Execute in the console directly
fusen::inflate(
  flat_file = "dev/data_preprocessing.Rmd",
  vignette_name = "Data preprocessing helpers"
)
```
